title: Resurrect auto-port reassignment, but for all providers
body:
- Move directory location code to break import cycles
- Updates common to main to pull in edit config locking

```release-note
none
```

comments.body: why do we have to move he machine code?
FWIW I don't have a particularly strong opinion on moving this machine code but am not convinced of the name of the new pkg.

> why do we have to move he machine code?

I needed some of these env methods to be reachable from machine/connection (GetDataDir/GetRuntimeDir etc) , which introduced a cycle. Since they have no natural dependency (just calculating paths of env vars / system constants) the cleanest approach was  to move them into a leaf package of some form. Although not hung up on the name.

Some more details in my other reply here:  https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/21927#discussion_r1511233410
I created https://github.com/containers/common/pull/1880 for the connections file lock.

@containers/podman-maintainers PTAL, should be good to go, other than it pulls in common@main. I reworked the commits to split off the env refactor into a separate commit, to make it easier to review (amounts to the same total delta but you can click each commit to breakout apart). I was going to offer to defer/drop the env refactor to post 5.0, but  Its still necessary for the port change since there is another cycle between vmconfig and machine. @Luap99 and I discussed this a bit in detail and he seems cool with it now: https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/21927#discussion_r1511421605

Im going to merge  but i wonder if any tests could be written for this? at least a regression test?  at least for windows?

/lgtm

> Im going to merge but i wonder if any tests could be written for this? at least a regression test? at least for windows?
> 
> /lgtm

Yeah sorry I should have addressed that. I plan to follow up with a regression test as part 2. Just wanted to get things in before blowing up your feature freeze.
